ocr.text: In the rapidly evolving landscape of e-commerce, the Gross Merchandise Volume (GMV) remains a critical metric for assessing
market performance and consumer engagement. As highlighted in the table, Alibaba Group's GMV trends for the second quarter
of 2025 indicate a robust growth trajectory, with a reported figure of €5.5B, representing a notable increase from €5.1B in the
same quarter of the previous year. This upsurge reflects a year-on-year growth rate of 38%, underscoring the resilience and
adaptability of the platform in a competitive environment. Notably, the performance of specific brands within the marketplace
has also contributed significantly to this growth.
Q22025  €5.5B  7.5%  36%  BrandA  BrandB  BrandC  Q22024  €5.1B  Q32025  €6.0B
BrandA  BrandB  BrandC  Q42024  €6.0B  [HIDDEN]  15%  AggregateGMV  €23.2B  Comparison  Total
For instance, BrandA's TotalGrowth metric illustrates the brand's substantial impact on overall sales performance, while BrandB
has shown promising trends, anticipating a strong performance leading into Q32024. The data reflects an optimistic outlook for
the upcoming quarters, as evidenced by projected figures of €6.0B for Q32025. This momentum is critical for stakeholders as
